steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence already has an opening em-dash before assuming: Walston's findings indicate that—assuming solar panel installers' ______ practice has the potential… A parenthetical aside opened by an em-dash must be closed by a matching em-dash before the main clause resumes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'cooperation this': No punctuation at all. The aside is never closed, so this practice cannot cleanly begin the main clause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'cooperation: this': A colon cannot close an em-dash aside — colons and em-dashes are not interchangeable as paired punctuation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'cooperation—this': Supplies the matching em-dash, correctly closing the aside —assuming solar panel installers' cooperation— so the main clause this practice has the potential… begins cleanly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'cooperation, this': A comma is too weak to close an em-dash aside; it creates a mismatched pair. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
